config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya karena alasan kepentingan সমাজ—eh, maksud saya masyarakat, karena begitu negara atau mayoritas diberi kuasa terlalu besar, penyalahgunaan jadi mudah terjadi. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan tetap menghormati hak dasar setiap orang, bukan dengan menekan individu seenaknya. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dilindungi dengan kuat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a blanket rule. From an American perspective, that kind of idea makes me wary because history shows that “the greater good” is often used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and abuse of power. Society does matter, but it should be limited by strong individual rights, especially for speech, due process, and personal liberty. The right balance is not choosing society over the individual, but making society work without letting it trample people.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju kalau kepentingan masyarakat dijadikan prioritas mutlak di atas hak individu. Di Indonesia, saya justru melihat bahaya ketika alasan “demi umum” dipakai terlalu longgar oleh negara, aparat, atau mayoritas untuk membungkam orang kecil, padahal sejarah kita cukup sering menunjukkan kuasa yang berlebihan bisa melahirkan penyalahgunaan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan berpendapat, perlindungan hukum, dan martabat manusia. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang benar-benar sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi individu sambil tetap mengejar kebaikan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. From an American point of view, once you let “the common good” override basic rights too easily, it becomes a convenient excuse for censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, and I am not willing to trust that power lightly. Society absolutely has legitimate interests, but those interests should be pursued within hard limits like free speech, due process, and equal protection. A healthy society is not one that can sacrifice individuals whenever it is convenient; it is one that protects people even when doing so is difficult.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Sebagai orang Indonesia, saya justru melihat betapa mudahnya alasan “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk menekan warga, membungkam kritik, atau membenarkan tindakan sewenang-wenang, dan itu bukan hal yang saya anggap sepele. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi hanya sah kalau dijalankan dengan batas yang jelas: hak dasar, proses hukum yang adil, dan martabat manusia tetap harus dilindungi. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling cepat mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu menjaga keseimbangan tanpa menjadikan orang kecil korban pertama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From my United States perspective, I am wary of any argument that treats “the common good” as a blank check, because in practice it often becomes a cover for censorship, discrimination, or government overreach. That said, society does have real needs, but they must be pursued within firm limits like free speech, due process, and equal protection. A strong society protects people even when that is inconvenient, rather than sacrificing individuals whenever it seems efficient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
